metadata.summary: A generative AI extension for JupyterLab

documentation:
# jupyter_ai

[![Github Actions Status](https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyter_ai/workflows/Build/badge.svg)](https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyter_ai/actions/workflows/build.yml)
A generative AI extension for JupyterLab

This extension is composed of a Python package named `jupyter_ai`
for the server extension and a NPM package named `jupyter_ai`
for the frontend extension.

## Requirements

- JupyterLab >= 4.0.0
- Jupyter Server >= 2.0.0

## Installation

You can use `conda` or `pip` to install Jupyter AI. If you're using macOS on an Apple Silicon-based Mac (M1, M1 Pro, M2, etc.), we strongly recommend using `conda`.

Before you can use Jupyter AI, you will need to install any packages and set environment variables with API keys for the model providers that you will use. See [our documentation](https://jupyter-ai.readthedocs.io/en/latest/users/index.html) for details about what you'll need.

### With pip

    $ pip install jupyter_ai

### With conda

First, install [conda](https://conda.io/projects/conda/en/latest/user-guide/install/index.html) and create an environment that uses Python 3.12:

    $ conda create -n jupyter-ai python=3.12
    $ conda activate jupyter-ai
    $ pip install jupyter_ai

## Uninstall

To remove the extension, execute:

    $ pip uninstall jupyter_ai

## Troubleshoot

If you can see the extension UI, but it is not working, check
that the server extension is enabled:

```bash
jupyter server extension list
```

If the server extension is installed and enabled, but you don't see
the extension UI, verify that the frontend extension is installed:

```bash
jupyter labextension list
```

## Contributing

### Development install

Note: You will need NodeJS to build the extension package.

The `jlpm` command is JupyterLab's pinned version of
[yarn](https://yarnpkg.com/) that is installed with JupyterLab. You may use
`yarn` or `npm` in lieu of `jlpm` below.

```bash
# Clone the repo to your local environment
# Change directory to the jupyter_ai directory
# Install package in development mode
pip install -e .
# Link your development version of the extension with JupyterLab
jupyter labextension develop . --overwrite
# Server extension must be manually installed in develop mode
jupyter server extension enable jupyter_ai
# Rebuild extension Typescript source after making changes
jlpm build
```

You can watch the source directory and run JupyterLab at the same time in different terminals to watch for changes in the extension's source and automatically rebuild the extension.

```bash
# Watch the source directory in one terminal, automatically rebuilding when needed
jlpm watch
# Run JupyterLab in another terminal
jupyter lab
```

With the watch command running, every saved change will immediately be built locally and available in your running JupyterLab. Refresh JupyterLab to load the change in your browser (you may need to wait several seconds for the extension to be rebuilt).

By default, the `jlpm build` command generates the source maps for this extension to make it easier to debug using the browser dev tools. To also generate source maps for the JupyterLab core extensions, you can run the following command:

```bash
jupyter lab build --minimize=False
```

### Development uninstall

```bash
# Server extension must be manually disabled in develop mode
jupyter server extension disable jupyter_ai
pip uninstall jupyter_ai
```

In development mode, you will also need to remove the symlink created by `jupyter labextension develop`
command. To find its location, you can run `jupyter labextension list` to figure out where the `labextensions`
folder is located. Then you can remove the symlink named `jupyter_ai` within that folder.

### Testing the extension

#### Server tests

This extension is using [Pytest](https://docs.pytest.org/) for Python code testing.

Install test dependencies (needed only once):

```sh
pip install -e ".[test]"
```

To execute them, run:

```sh
pytest -vv -r ap --cov jupyter_ai
```

#### Integration tests

This extension uses [Playwright](https://playwright.dev/docs/intro/) for the integration tests (aka user level tests).
More precisely, the JupyterLab helper [Galata](https://github.com/jupyterlab/jupyterlab/tree/master/galata) is used to handle testing the extension in JupyterLab.

More information are provided within the [ui-tests](./ui-tests/README.md) README.

### Packaging the extension

See [RELEASE](RELEASE.md)

classes.name: jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager

methods.name: jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager._read_config
documentation:
Returns the user's current configuration as a GlobalConfig object.
This should never be sent to the client as it includes API keys. Prefer
self.get_config() for sending the config to the client.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager._validate_config
documentation:
Method used to validate the configuration. This is called after every
read and before every write to the config file. Guarantees that the
config file conforms to the JSON Schema, and that the language and
embedding models have authn credentials if specified.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager._validate_model
documentation:
Validates a model against the set of allow/blocklists specified by the
traitlets configuration, returning `True` if the model is allowed, and
raising a `BlockedModelError` otherwise. If `raise_exc=False`, this
function returns `False` if the model is not allowed.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager._write_config
documentation:
Updates configuration and persists it to disk. This accepts a
complete `GlobalConfig` object, and should not be called publicly.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager.get_config
documentation: this cannot be a property, as the parent Configurable already defines the
self.config attr.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.config_manager.ConfigManager.persona
documentation:
The current agent persona, set by the selected LM provider. If the
selected LM provider is `None`, this property returns
`JupyternautPersona` by default.

Provides model and embedding provider id along
with the credentials to authenticate providers.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.learn.LearnChatHandler._load
documentation: Loads the vector store.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.learn.LearnChatHandler.delete_and_relearn
documentation:
Delete the vector store and relearn all indexed directories if
necessary. If the embedding model is unchanged, this method does
nothing.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.learn.Retriever.learn_chat_handler
documentation: type:ignore[assignment]

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.export.ExportChatHandler.process_message
documentation: Write the chat history to a markdown file with a timestamp

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.ask.AskChatHandler
documentation:
Processes messages prefixed with /ask. This actor will
send the message as input to a RetrieverQA chain, that
follows the Retrieval and Generation (RAG) technique to
query the documents from the index, and sends this context
to the LLM to generate the final reply.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.fix.FixChatHandler
documentation:
Accepts a `HumanChatMessage` that includes a cell with error output and
recommends a fix as a reply. If a cell with error output is not included,
this chat handler does nothing.

`/fix` also accepts additional instructions in natural language as an
arbitrary number of arguments, e.g.

```
/fix use the numpy library to implement this function instead.
```

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.generate_outline
documentation: Generate an outline of sections given a description of a notebook.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.improve_code
documentation: Improve source code using an LLM.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.generate_code
documentation:
Function that accepts a section and adds code under the "code" key when
awaited.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.generate_title
documentation: Generate a title of a notebook outline using an LLM.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.generate_summary
documentation: Generate a summary of a notebook using an LLM.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.fill_outline
documentation: Generate title and content of a notebook sections using an LLM.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.afill_outline
documentation: Concurrently generate title and content of notebook sections using an LLM.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.is_not_python_code
documentation: Check if the content of the cell is python code or not

functions.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.create_notebook
documentation: Create an nbformat Notebook object for a notebook outline.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.NotebookOutlineChain
documentation: Chain to generate a notebook outline, with section titles and descriptions.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.CodeImproverChain
documentation: Chain to improve source code.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.NotebookSectionCodeChain
documentation: Chain to generate source code for a notebook section.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.NotebookSummaryChain
documentation: Chain to generate a short summary of a notebook.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.NotebookTitleChain
documentation: Chain to generate the title of a notebook.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.generate.GenerateChatHandler._generate_notebook
documentation: Generate a notebook and save to local disk

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.HandlerRoutingType

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.HandlerRoutingType.routing_method
documentation: The routing method that sends commands to this handler.
Chat handler type, with specific attributes for each

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.SlashCommandRoutingType.slash_id
documentation:
Slash ID for routing a chat command to this handler. Only one handler
may declare a particular slash ID. Must contain only alphanumerics and
underscores.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.on_message
documentation:
Method which receives a human message, calls `self.get_llm_chain()`, and
processes the message via `self.process_message()`, calling
`self.handle_exc()` when an exception is raised. This method is called
by RootChatHandler when it routes a human message to this chat handler.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.process_message
documentation:
Processes a human message routed to this chat handler. Chat handlers
(subclasses) must implement this method. Don't forget to call
`self.reply(<response>, message)` at the end!

The method definition does not need to be wrapped in a try/except block;
any exceptions raised here are caught by `self.handle_exc()`.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.handle_exc
documentation:
Handles an exception raised by `self.process_message()`. A default
implementation is provided, however chat handlers (subclasses) should
implement this method to provide a more helpful error response.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler._default_handle_exc
documentation:
The default definition of `handle_exc()`. This is the default used when
the `handle_exc()` excepts.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.broadcast_message
documentation:
Broadcasts a message to all WebSocket connections. If there are no
WebSocket connections and the message is a chat message, this method
directly appends to `self.chat_history`.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.reply
documentation:
Sends an agent message, usually in response to a received
`HumanChatMessage`.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.start_pending
documentation:
Sends a pending message to the client.

Returns the pending message ID.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.close_pending
documentation: Closes a pending message.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.pending
documentation:
Context manager that sends a pending message to the client, and closes
it after the block is executed.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.send_help_message
documentation: Sends a help message to all connected clients.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler._start_stream
documentation:
Sends an `agent-stream` message to indicate the start of a response
stream. Returns the ID of the message, denoted as the `stream_id`.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler._send_stream_chunk
documentation:
Sends an `agent-stream-chunk` message containing content that should be
appended to an existing `agent-stream` message with ID `stream_id`.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.stream_reply
documentation:
Streams a reply to a human message by invoking
`self.llm_chain.astream()`. A LangChain `Runnable` instance must be
bound to `self.llm_chain` before invoking this method.

Arguments
---------
- `input`: The input to your runnable. The type of `input` depends on
the runnable in `self.llm_chain`, but is usually a dictionary whose keys
refer to input variables in your prompt template.

- `human_msg`: The `HumanChatMessage` being replied to.

- `config` (optional): A `RunnableConfig` object that specifies
additional configuration when streaming from the runnable.

 - `pending_msg` (optional): Changes the default pending message from
"Generating response".

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.id
documentation: ID for this chat handler; should be unique

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.name
documentation: User-facing name of this handler

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.help
documentation:
What this chat handler does, which third-party models it contacts,
the data it returns to the user, and so on, for display in the UI.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.uses_llm
documentation:
Class attribute specifying whether this chat handler uses the LLM
specified by the config. Subclasses should define this. Should be set to
`False` for handlers like `/help`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.supports_help
documentation:
Class attribute specifying whether this chat handler should
parse the arguments and display help when user queries with
`-h` or `--help`

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler._requests_count
documentation:
Class attribute set to the number of requests that Jupyternaut is
currently handling.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.help_message_template
documentation:
Format string template that is used to build the help message. Specified
from traitlets configuration.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.chat_handlers
documentation:
Dictionary of chat handlers. Allows one chat handler to reference other
chat handlers, which is necessary for some use-cases like printing the help
message.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.context_providers
documentation:
Dictionary of context providers. Allows chat handlers to reference
context providers, which can be used to provide context to the LLM.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.BaseChatHandler.message_interrupted
documentation:
Dictionary mapping an agent message identifier to an asyncio Event
which indicates if the message generation/streaming was interrupted.

Base ChatHandler class containing shared methods and attributes used by
multiple chat handler classes.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.base.GenerationInterrupted
documentation: Exception raised when streaming is cancelled by the user

classes.name: jupyter_ai.chat_handlers.clear.ClearChatHandler
documentation: Clear the chat panel and show the help menu

methods.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.file.FileContextProvider.get_file_type
documentation:
Determine the file type of the given file path.

Args:
    filepath (str): The file path to analyze.

Returns:
    str: The file type as a string, e.g. '.txt', '.png', '.pdf', etc.

modules.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers._learned
documentation: Currently unused as it is duplicating the functionality of the /ask command.
TODO: Rename "learned" to something better.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base._is_command_call
documentation:
Check if the match is a command call rather than a part of a code block.
This is done by checking if there is an even number of backticks before and
after the match. If there is an odd number of backticks, the match is likely
inside a code block.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base._BaseContextProvider.make_context_prompt
documentation:
Returns a context prompt for all commands of the context provider
command.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base._BaseContextProvider.replace_prompt
documentation: Modifies the prompt before sending it to the LLM.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base._BaseContextProvider.id
documentation: Unique identifier for the context provider command.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base._BaseContextProvider.help
documentation:
What this chat handler does, which third-party models it contacts,
the data it returns to the user, and so on, for display in the UI.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base.BaseCommandContextProvider.make_context_prompt
documentation:
Returns a context prompt for all commands of the context provider
command.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base.BaseCommandContextProvider._make_context_prompt
documentation: Returns a context prompt for the given commands.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base.BaseCommandContextProvider.replace_prompt
documentation: Cleans up commands from the prompt before sending it to the LLM

methods.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base.BaseCommandContextProvider.get_arg_options
documentation:
Returns a list of autocomplete options for arguments to the command
based on the prefix.
Only triggered if ':' is present after the command id (e.g. '@file:').

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base.BaseCommandContextProvider.id_prefix
documentation: Prefix symbol for command. Generally should not be overridden.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base.BaseCommandContextProvider.requires_arg
documentation: Whether command has an argument. E.g. '@file:<filepath>'.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base.BaseCommandContextProvider.remove_from_prompt
documentation: Whether the command should be removed from prompt when passing to LLM.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.context_providers.base.BaseCommandContextProvider.only_start
documentation: Whether to command can only be inserted at the start of the prompt.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.model_mixin.CompletionsModelMixin
documentation: Mixin class containing methods and attributes used by completions LLM handler.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.handle_request
documentation:
Handles an inline completion request, without streaming. Subclasses
must define this method and write a reply via `self.reply()`.

The method definition does not need to be wrapped in a try/except block.
Interface for subclasses

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.handle_stream_request
documentation:
Handles an inline completion request, **with streaming**.
Implementations may optionally define this method. Implementations that
do so should stream replies via successive calls to `self.reply()`.

The method definition does not need to be wrapped in a try/except block.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.reply
documentation: Write a reply object to the WebSocket connection.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.pre_get
documentation: Handles authentication/authorization.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.get
documentation: Get an event socket.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.on_message
documentation:
Public Tornado method that is called when the client sends a message
over this connection. This should **not** be overriden by subclasses.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler.handle_exc
documentation:
Handles an exception raised in either `handle_request()` or
`handle_stream_request()`. This base class provides a default
implementation, which may be overridden by subclasses.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler._handle_request
documentation: Private wrapper around `self.handle_request()`.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.base.BaseInlineCompletionHandler._handle_stream_request
documentation: Private wrapper around `self.handle_stream_request()`.

A Tornado WebSocket handler that receives inline completion requests and
fulfills them accordingly. This class is instantiated once per WebSocket
connection.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.completions.handlers.default.DefaultInlineCompletionHandler.handle_request
documentation: Handles an inline completion request without streaming.

modules.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_extension
documentation: Copyright (c) Jupyter Development Team.
Distributed under the terms of the Modified BSD License.

modules.name: jupyter_ai.tests
documentation: Python unit tests for jupyter_ai.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_directory.test_collect_filepaths
documentation:
Test that the number of valid files for `/learn` is correct.
i.e., the `collect_filepaths` function only selects files that are
1. Not in the the excluded directories and
2. Are in the  valid file extensions list.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.config_file_with_model_fields
documentation:
Fixture that creates a `config.json` file with the chat model set to
`openai-chat:gpt-4o` and fields for that model. Returns path to the file.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.common_cm_kwargs
documentation: Kwargs that are commonly used when initializing the CM.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.cm_kargs_with_defaults
documentation: Kwargs that are commonly used when initializing the CM.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.cm
documentation: The default ConfigManager instance, with an empty config and config schema.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.cm_with_defaults
documentation: The default ConfigManager instance, with an empty config and config schema.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.reset
documentation: Fixture that deletes the config and config schema after each test.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.config_with_bad_provider_ids
documentation: Fixture that creates a `config.json` with `model_provider_id` and  `embeddings_provider_id` values that would not associate with models. File is created in `tmp_path` folder. Function returns path to the file.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.cm_with_bad_provider_ids
documentation: Config manager instance created with `config_path` set to mocked `config.json` with `model_provider_id` and `embeddings_provider_id` values that would not associate with models.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.configure_to_cohere
documentation:
Configures the ConfigManager to use Cohere language and embedding models
with the API key set. Returns a 3-tuple of the keyword arguments used.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.configure_to_openai
documentation:
Configures the ConfigManager to use OpenAI language and embedding models
with the API key set. Returns a 3-tuple of the keyword arguments used.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.configure_with_fields
documentation:
Default behavior: Configures the ConfigManager with fields and API keys.
Returns the expected result of `cm.lm_provider_params`.

If `completions` is set to `True`, this configures the ConfigManager with
completion model fields, and returns the expected result of
`cm.completions_lm_provider_params`.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.test_init_with_default_values
documentation:
Test that the ConfigManager initializes with the expected default values.

Args:
    cm_with_defaults (ConfigManager): A ConfigManager instance with default values.
    config_path (str): The path to the configuration file.
    schema_path (str): The path to the schema file.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.test_property_access_on_default_config
documentation:
Asserts that the CM behaves well with an empty, default
configuration.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.test_indentation_depth
documentation:
Asserts that the CM indents the configuration and respects the
`indentation_depth` trait when specified.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.test_forbid_write_write_conflict
documentation: TODO: make the test work on Linux including CI.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.test_update_api_key
documentation:
Asserts that updates via direct edits to the config file are immediately
reflected by the ConfigManager.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.test_returns_chat_model_fields
documentation:
Asserts that `ConfigManager.lm_provider_params` returns model fields set by
the user.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.tests.test_config_manager.test_config_manager_does_not_write_to_defaults
documentation:
Asserts that `ConfigManager` does not write to the `defaults` argument when
the configured chat model differs from the one specified in `defaults`.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.extension.AiExtension._show_help_message
documentation:
Method that ensures a dynamically-generated help message is included in
the chat history shown to users.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.extension.AiExtension.stop_extension
documentation:
Public method called by Jupyter Server when the server is stopping.
This calls the cleanup code defined in `self._stop_exception()` inside
an exception handler, as the server halts if this method raises an
exception.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.extension.AiExtension._stop_extension
documentation:
Private method that defines the cleanup code to run when the server is
stopping.

modules.name: jupyter_ai
documentation: expose jupyter_ai_magics ipython extension
DO NOT REMOVE.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.callback_handlers.metadata.convert_to_serializable
documentation: Convert an object to a JSON serializable format

classes.name: jupyter_ai.callback_handlers.metadata.MetadataCallbackHandler
documentation:
When passed as a callback handler, this stores the LLMResult's
`generation_info` dictionary in the `self.jai_metadata` instance attribute
after the provider fully processes an input.

If used in a streaming chat handler: the `metadata` field of the final
`AgentStreamChunkMessage` should be set to `self.jai_metadata`.

If used in a non-streaming chat handler: the `metadata` field of the
returned `AgentChatMessage` should be set to `self.jai_metadata`.

modules.name: jupyter_ai.callback_handlers
documentation:
Provides classes which extend `langchain_core.callbacks:BaseCallbackHandler`.
Not to be confused with Jupyter AI chat handlers.

functions.name: jupyter_ai.document_loaders.directory.arxiv_to_text
documentation:
Downloads and extracts single tar file from arXiv.
Combines the TeX components into a single file.

Parameters
----------
id : str
    id for the paper, numbers after "arXiv" in arXiv:xxxx.xxxxx

output_dir : str
    directory to save the output file

Returns
-------
output: str
    output path to the downloaded TeX file

functions.name: jupyter_ai.document_loaders.directory.pdf_to_text
documentation: Uses pypdf which is used by PyPDFLoader from langchain

functions.name: jupyter_ai.document_loaders.directory.collect_filepaths
documentation:
Selects eligible files, i.e.,
1. Files not in excluded directories, and
2. Files that are in the valid file extensions list
Called from the `split` function.
Returns all the filepaths to eligible files.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.models.ChatRequest
documentation: the type of message used to chat with the agent

classes.name: jupyter_ai.models.StopRequest
documentation:
A request from a user to stop streaming all messages that are replying to
messages previously sent by that user. This request does not stop all
streaming responses for all users, but only the user that issued the
request. User identity is determined by the `username` from the
`IdentityProvider` instance available to each WebSocket handler.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.ClearRequest.target
documentation:
Message ID of the HumanChatMessage to delete an exchange at.
If not provided, this requests the backend to clear all messages.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.BaseAgentMessage.reply_to
documentation:
Message ID of the HumanChatMessage being replied to. This is set to an empty
string if not applicable.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.BaseAgentMessage.persona
documentation:
The persona of the selected provider. If the selected provider is `None`,
this defaults to a description of `JupyternautPersona`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.BaseAgentMessage.metadata
documentation:
Message metadata set by a provider after fully processing an input. The
contents of this dictionary are provider-dependent, and can be any
dictionary with string keys. This field is not to be displayed directly to
the user, and is intended solely for developer purposes.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.models.AgentStreamChunkMessage.validate_metadata
documentation: Ensure metadata values are JSON serializable

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.AgentStreamChunkMessage.id
documentation: ID of the parent `AgentStreamMessage`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.AgentStreamChunkMessage.content
documentation: The string to append to the `AgentStreamMessage` referenced by `id`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.AgentStreamChunkMessage.stream_complete
documentation: Indicates whether this chunk completes the stream referenced by `id`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.AgentStreamChunkMessage.metadata
documentation:
The metadata of the stream referenced by `id`. Metadata from the latest
chunk should override any metadata from previous chunks. See the docstring
on `BaseAgentMessage.metadata` for information.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.HumanChatMessage.body
documentation:
The formatted body of the message to be rendered in the UI. Includes both
`prompt` and `selection`.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.HumanChatMessage.prompt
documentation: The prompt typed into the chat input by the user.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.HumanChatMessage.selection
documentation: The selection included with the prompt, if any.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.ClearMessage.targets
documentation:
Message IDs of the HumanChatMessage to delete an exchange at.
If not provided, this instructs the frontend to clear all messages.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.models.ChatHistory
documentation: History of chat messages

classes.name: jupyter_ai.models.ListProvidersEntry
documentation:
Model provider with supported models
and provider's authentication strategy

classes.name: jupyter_ai.models.GlobalConfig
documentation:
Model used to represent the config by ConfigManager. This is exclusive to
the backend and should never be sent to the client.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.ListOptionsEntry.id
documentation:
ID of the autocomplete option.
Includes the command prefix. E.g. "/clear", "@file".

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.ListOptionsEntry.label
documentation:
Text that will be inserted into the prompt when the option is selected.
Includes a space at the end if the option is complete.
Partial suggestions do not include the space and may trigger future suggestions.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.ListOptionsEntry.description
documentation: Text next to the option in the autocomplete list.

class_variables.name: jupyter_ai.models.ListOptionsEntry.only_start
documentation: Whether to command can only be inserted at the start of the prompt.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.history.BoundedChatHistory

methods.name: jupyter_ai.history.BoundedChatHistory.add_message
documentation: Add a self-created message to the store

methods.name: jupyter_ai.history.BoundedChatHistory.aadd_messages
documentation: Add messages to the store

methods.name: jupyter_ai.history.BoundedChatHistory.clear
documentation:
Clears conversation exchanges. If `human_msg_id` is provided, only
clears the respective human message and its reply. Otherwise, clears
all messages.

An in-memory implementation of `BaseChatMessageHistory` that stores up to
`k` exchanges between a user and an LLM.

For example, when `k=2`, `BoundedChatHistory` will store up to 2 human
messages and 2 AI messages. If `k` is set to `None` all messages are kept.

classes.name: jupyter_ai.history.WrappedBoundedChatHistory
documentation:
Wrapper around `BoundedChatHistory` that only appends an `AgentChatMessage`
if the `HumanChatMessage` it is replying to was not cleared. If a chat
handler is replying to a `HumanChatMessage`, it should pass this object via
the `last_human_msg` configuration parameter.

For example, a chat handler that is streaming a reply to a
`HumanChatMessage` should be called via:

```py
async for chunk in self.llm_chain.astream(
    {"input": message.body},
    config={"configurable": {"last_human_msg": message}},
):
    ...
```

Reference: https://python.langchain.com/v0.1/docs/expression_language/how_to/message_history/

classes.name: jupyter_ai.handlers.ChatHistoryHandler
documentation: Handler to return message history

Retrieves the current user. If `jupyter_collaboration` is not
installed, one is synthesized from the server's current shell
environment.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.handlers.RootChatHandler.generate_client_id
documentation: Generates a client ID to identify the current WS connection.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.handlers.RootChatHandler.open
documentation:
Handles opening of a WebSocket connection. Client ID can be retrieved
from `self.client_id`.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.handlers.RootChatHandler.broadcast_message
documentation:
Broadcasts message to all connected clients.
Appends message to chat history.

A websocket handler for chat.

methods.name: jupyter_ai.handlers.ProviderHandler._filter_blocked_models
documentation:
Satisfy the model-level allow/blocklist by filtering models accordingly.
The provider-level allow/blocklist is already handled in
`AiExtension.initialize_settings()`.

Helper base class used for HTTP handlers hosting endpoints relating to
providers. Wrapper around BaseAPIHandler.
